Here's what I want. 

Write me an outline for a screen play of episode one for a "Marylin Monster" spin off series. Feel free to assign any name that is appropriate to the series and episode.

Imagine a Fan Fiction spin off series where the primary POV character is Marilyn. 

We follow her as an adult with a "normal" life, husband, job, and a daughter in school. We open with just enough world building to recognize

It opens with a Hook: Marylin encountering something WEIRD that her coworkers find shocking (either a thing or person?).

Set-up: After the hook, we find just enough world building to realize she's "that Marilyn from The Monsters", maybe some photos in the background or a FaceTime call with mom and Dad at home, and a reference to uncle and brother.

Then, some kind of episode one situation that would show her being the calm in the storm while the world around her is freaking out, all because she came from this unique family. 

End with some kind of take away about how it's possibly beneficial to be Neurodivergent. </div></div></div><div class="mt-1 flex justify-start gap-3 empty:hidden" style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; (desperate)

1.25em;">Absolutely, Darrell. Here's a short story adaptation of the screenplay excerpt for "Marilyn's World: Unexpected Normalcy."</p><hr style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: var(--tw-prose-hr); border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: solid; box-sizing: border-box; color: inherit; height: 0px; margin: 3em 0px;" /><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0px 0px 1.25em;">Marilyn sipped her coffee in the office break room, a sanctuary of normalcy amidst her unusual heritage. The room buzzed with the mundane chatter of her coworkers, a stark contrast to the eccentric conversations at her family's dinner table. As the lights flickered and a ghostly whisper caressed the room, her colleagues erupted in panic. Marilyn, however, merely smiled, her calm demeanor a testament to a childhood where the extraordinary was ordinary.</p><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 1.25em 0px;">Later, at her home, which blended contemporary comfort with odd, Munster-esque relics, Marilyn chatted with her parents, Lily and Herman, over a video call. Their grainy images filled the screen, exuding warmth and quirkiness. Lily's inquiries about Marilyn's day and Herman's humorous jabs about the 'normal' world reminded her of the bridge she straddled between two worlds.</p><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 1.25em 0px;">Back at the office, a crisis loomed. A major project had hit a snag, sending waves of panic through the cubicles. As her coworkers floundered, Marilyn's unique upbringing kicked in. She approached the challenge with a blend of Munster resourcefulness and modern practicality, calmly navigating the chaos. Her fingers danced over the keyboard, weaving an improvised yet brilliant solution out of the digital mire.</p><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 1.25em 0px;">In the conference room, Marilyn's presentation captivated her colleagues. Her ability to transform a near-disaster into a triumph earned their admiration and awe. As they marveled at her composure, Marilyn's thoughts drifted to her family. The Munsters had taught her that even in the face of the bizarre and unexpected, one could find a way to prevail.</p><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 1.25em 0px;">That evening, surrounded by her loving husband and curious daughter in their cozy living room, Marilyn reflected on the day's events. She imparted a lesson to her daughter, a blend of Munster wisdom and worldly insight. "Being different," she mused, "gives us a unique view of the world." It was a view that allowed her to see solutions where others saw only problems, to find calm in chaos, and to embrace the unexpected with a smile.</p><hr style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: var(--tw-prose-hr); border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: solid; box-sizing: border-box; color: inherit; height: 0px; margin: 3em 0px;" /><p style="--tw-border-spacing-x: 0; --tw-border-spacing-y: 0; --tw-ring-color: rgba(69,89,164,.5); --tw-ring-offset-color: #fff; --tw-ring-offset-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-ring-offset-width: 0px; --tw-ring-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-rotate: 0; --tw-scale-x: 1; --tw-scale-y: 1; --tw-scroll-snap-strictness: proximity; --tw-shadow-colored: 0 0 transparent; --tw-shadow: 0 0 transparent; --tw-skew-x: 0; --tw-skew-y: 0; --tw-translate-x: 0; --tw-translate-y: 0; border: 0px solid rgb(217, 217, 227); box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0px;">This adaptation captures the essence of the screenplay in a narrative form, focusing on "show, don't tell" to bring Marilyn's world to life. Let's use the KM Weiland 9 story points (Hook, Inciting Event, Plot Point 1, Pinch Point 1, Midpoint (aka Plot Point 2), Pinch Point 2, Plot Point 3, Climactic Moment, Resolution. Let's also use the concept of fail cycles (taught by Mary Robinette Kowal) where the character fails in a series of "yes, but" or "no, and" cycles resolving on a "yes, and" and tying tge resolution to the opening.
